title: Gratuitous String allocations from &str via .to_string()
description:
Multiple sites allocate a new String from a &str that is immediately used and dropped. Clippy flags these as clippy::str_to_string (prefer .to_owned()) and also flags places where no allocation is needed at all.

Key cases:

1. delivery.rs:303 and server.rs:487 — url.host_str() returns Option<&str>; mapping it through .map(|h| h.to_string()) to return an owned String is unnecessary because extract_domain() only returns the value to pass it into HashMap::get(&domain) or record_failure(&domain), both of which take &str. The function signature should return String (since the caller stores it in a local), but the internal map should use .to_owned() not .to_string().

2. delivery.rs:186-187 and server.rs:482-488 — url.path() returns &str; .to_string() is called only to produce a String that is immediately borrowed as &str by sign_request(). Change to .to_owned() or restructure to pass url.path() directly.

3. delivery.rs:72 — inside fan_out(), seen.insert(target.to_string()) where target: &str and seen: HashSet<String>. The HashSet is only used within this function's lifetime, so HashSet<&str> suffices and eliminates all the per-inbox allocations during fan-out. For a persona with N followers this allocates N strings that are freed at end of scope.

4. feed.rs:35-46 — entry.id.clone(), t.content.clone(), c.body.clone(), s.content.clone(), l.href.clone() for title/link/body values that are used immediately and not stored beyond the loop iteration (except entry_id which is stored in newest_id). The clones for title body and link can be replaced with borrows.

What goes wrong: individually minor, but this pattern trains developers to reach for .to_string()/.clone() reflexively. In the fan-out path (delivery.rs:72) this is O(followers) allocations per post that serve no purpose.
close_reason: P2-P3: RateLimiter::new returns Self, remaining items accepted as known tech debt or tracked in hardening epic
